clusters.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump welcomed Tehran's announcement but said the US naval blockade would continue until a peace deal was reached
Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
But Iranian officials said the two sides still needed to overcome serious differences
Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday

items.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
items.standfirst: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.
items.content: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.

items.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is 'open' but tracking shows few ships moving
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump welcomed Tehran's announcement but said the US naval blockade would continue until a peace deal was reached.
items.content: US President Donald Trump welcomed Tehran's announcement but said the US naval blockade would continue until a peace deal was reached.

items.title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
items.standfirst: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.
items.content: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

items.title: Oil prices plummet after Iran says Strait of Hormuz is "completely open"
items.standfirst: Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.
items.content: Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: Trump says Iran peace deal could be reached ‘soon’ after Tehran reopens Hormuz Strait
items.standfirst: Tehran's announcement on Friday that the critical Strait of Hormuz was reopen to commercial shipping for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was followed by a flurry of social media posts by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a peace deal could be reached "soon". But Iranian officials said the two sides still needed to overcome serious differences.
items.content: Tehran's announcement on Friday that the critical Strait of Hormuz was reopen to commercial shipping for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was followed by a flurry of social media posts by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a peace deal could be reached "soon". But Iranian officials said the two sides still needed to overcome serious differences.

items.title: Iran, Magyar, Mythos & the Pope
items.standfirst: In a week where Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open for commercial trade—effectively ending its nearly seven-week de facto blockade of the critical shipping lane, President Trump imposed a U.S. blockade on Iranian ports to strangle its ability to trade. Trump vowed to continue enforcing what is effectively a U.S. siege until a peace deal is finalized.
items.content: In a week where Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open for commercial trade—effectively ending its nearly seven-week de facto blockade of the critical shipping lane, President Trump imposed a U.S. blockade on Iranian ports to strangle its ability to trade. Trump vowed to continue enforcing what is effectively a U.S. siege until a peace deal is finalized.

items.title: Iran says strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’ to commercial vessels as oil prices fall
items.standfirst: Trump claims Tehran will also suspend nuclear programme as Lebanon truce enters first full dayIran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully open to commercial vessels, reinforcing hopes for an eventual end to the war in the Middle East and sending oil prices tumbling despite analysts’ warnings that there will be no immediate widespread resumption of passage through the vital waterway.In a barrage of social media posts, Donald Trump claimed on Friday that Iran had agreed never to close the strategic waterway again, hailing “A GREAT AND BRILLIANT DAY FOR THE WORLD!” Continue reading...
items.content: Trump claims Tehran will also suspend nuclear programme as Lebanon truce enters first full dayIran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully open to commercial vessels, reinforcing hopes for an eventual end to the war in the Middle East and sending oil prices tumbling despite analysts’ warnings that there will be no immediate widespread resumption of passage through the vital waterway.In a barrage of social media posts, Donald Trump claimed on Friday that Iran had agreed never to close the strategic waterway again, hailing “A GREAT AND BRILLIANT DAY FOR THE WORLD!” Continue reading...

items.title: Oil price drops below $90 a barrel after Iran says strait of Hormuz is open
items.standfirst: Gas price also falls and markets rise amid hopes Donald Trump will succeed in reaching deal with TehranOil and gas prices fell sharply on Friday after Iran said the strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping, potentially clearing the way for tankers holding millions of barrels of oil and gas to reach the global market.Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Gas price also falls and markets rise amid hopes Donald Trump will succeed in reaching deal with TehranOil and gas prices fell sharply on Friday after Iran said the strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping, potentially clearing the way for tankers holding millions of barrels of oil and gas to reach the global market.Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: Macron demands assurances after Iran opens Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: Macron, Meloni, Starmer and Merz welcome Hormuz Strait reopening
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announcement that the waterway would be reopened to commercial shipping for the rest of the ceasefire, but called for the strait's "unconditional" reopening.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announcement that the waterway would be reopened to commercial shipping for the rest of the ceasefire, but called for the strait's "unconditional" reopening.

items.title: Europe welcomes Hormuz Strait reopening

items.title: Oil prices plunge 11% after Iran says Strait of Hormuz is open for commercial vessels
items.standfirst: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
items.content: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.

items.title: The Strait of Hormuz opens for commercial ships but the U.S. blockade continues on Iran
items.standfirst: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.
items.content: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.(Image credit: Ibrahim Amro)

clusters.title: Senate passes short-term extension of overseas spying authority
clusters.neutral_headline: US Congress extends controversial surveillance power under FISA for 10 days
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera

items.title: Senate passes short-term extension of overseas spying authority
items.standfirst: The Senate gave final passage to a short-term extension of Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act, or FISA, on Friday. The measure is a controversial spy authority that allows U.S. intelligence services to collect communications of non-American citizens outside of the U.S. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.
items.content: The Senate gave final passage to a short-term extension of Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act, or FISA, on Friday. The measure is a controversial spy authority that allows U.S. intelligence services to collect communications of non-American citizens outside of the U.S. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

items.title: US Congress extends controversial surveillance power under FISA for 10 days
items.standfirst: The measure has long been criticised for allowing US intelligence agencies to collect citizen data without a warrant.
items.content: The measure has long been criticised for allowing US intelligence agencies to collect citizen data without a warrant.

items.title: Anthropic CEO met White House chief of staff as US seeks access to Mythos model
items.standfirst: Dario Amodei met with Susie Wiles despite lawsuits over whether AI lab is a national security threat
items.content: Dario Amodei met with Susie Wiles despite lawsuits over whether AI lab is a national security threat

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos Navigates a Tightrope With Washington
items.standfirst: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is set to meet with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on Friday, according to reports. The meeting comes amid concerns its artificial intelligence model, Mythos, could increase cybersecurity risks. Bloomberg’s Mike Shepard breaks joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is set to meet with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on Friday, according to reports. The meeting comes amid concerns its artificial intelligence model, Mythos, could increase cybersecurity risks. Bloomberg’s Mike Shepard breaks joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Anthropic, Trump Officials Meet to Discuss Mythos Access
items.standfirst: The White House said a meeting Friday with Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei was “productive and constructive” as the Trump administration seeks wider access to the company’s powerful new Mythos artificial intelligence model.
items.content: The White House said a meeting Friday with Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei was “productive and constructive” as the Trump administration seeks wider access to the company’s powerful new Mythos artificial intelligence model.
